rulings.Case Title: IN RE: BAY AREA QUALITY MANAGEMENT DISTRICT EMPLOYEES'
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 21, 2018, Line 1, PETITIONER BAY AREA QUALITY MANAGEMENT DISTRICT EMPLOYEES' Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration. Petitioner Bay Area Air Quality Management District Employees' Association's petition to compel arbitration and to consolidate separate arbitration proceedings is granted. Respondent Bay Area Quality Management District is required to arbitrate the two grievances. The two grievances are consolidated, although the arbitrator is authorized to sever them or make any other appropriate orders regarding phasing of the issues. The delegation clause in section 4.12.2 of the MOU is clear and unmistakable and requires that any disputes regarding arbitrability are to be decided by the arbitrator. The issue whether the aggrieved workers in the two grievances are "represented employees" is an issue about arbitrability, which is within the scope of the delegation clause. The two grievances arise from the same series of related transactions and involve common issues of law or fact and are thus consolidated per CCP 1281.3. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the EA is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TERRY WILLIAM IRELAND et al VS. GORDON & REES LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Orders
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 21, 2018, Line 3, PLAINTIFF (CONSOLIDATED CASE) DAVID FELDMAN'S, Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Orders. Plaintiff David Feldman's motion to vacate orders and judgments is denied in its entirety. While there appears to be little doubt that Mr. Feldman and those he is allied with in these consolidated cases were victims of egregious misconduct committed by their counsel of record Robert Machado, Mr. Feldman is not entitled to any of the relief that he seeks. Mr. Feldman's contention that the orders and judgments he seeks to have vacated are "void" and thus can be vacated at any time per CCP 473(d) is incorrect. This court has and continues to have fundamental jurisdiction over the parties and the subject matter of this case. Per longstanding law explained in Lee v. Ji Hae An (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 558, the orders and judgments sought to be vacated are at most "voidable," triggering the 6 month period of CCP 473(b) and precluding application of CCP 473(d). Quite obviously Mr. Feldman failed to file this motion within six months of any of the orders and judgments he seeks to have vacated. Nor may Mr. Feldman invoke extrinsic fraud to set aside any of the orders or judgments. The law of the case doctrine requires that this court follow all the rulings in the recent Court of Appeal decision. Among those rulings is that the orders and judgments were not procured by extrinsic fraud because there was "strong evidence" that Mr. Machado was solely responsible for the evidentiary sanctions orders. The Court of Appeal also ruled that Mr. Feldman was not free from negligence because he enlisted Mr. Morrissey, who was suspended from the practice of law, to work with Mr. Machado on behalf of Mr. Feldman and the other plaintiffs. Although the law of the case doctrine may not apply when there has been a material change in circumstances, the 2017 convictions of Mr. Morrissey, Mr. Machado and Ms. McCarroll do not constitute a material change in circumstances because nothing about those convictions casts doubt on or requires reconsideration of the previously mentioned rulings of the Court of Appeal. Indeed, fairly read, in his declaration submitted on this motion, Mr. Machado still takes responsibility for the discovery sanctions orders and, also fairly read, Mr. Feldman's declaration on this motion re-affirms his participation in an arrangement where Mr. Morrissey, whom he knew to be an attorney not able to practice law, would work together with Mr. Machado on his and the other plaintiffs' behalf. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for Ropers is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TERRY WILLIAM IRELAND et al VS. GORDON & REES LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Disqualify Carlson, Calladine & Peterson Llp
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 21, 2018, Line 3, PLAINTIFF DAVID FELDMAN'S Motion To Disqualify Carlson, Calladine & Peterson LLP. Plaintiff David Feldman's motion to disqualify Carlson, Calladine & Peterson LLP is denied. Assuming without deciding that this court has jurisdiction to decide this motion and there will be ongoing proceedings in this case which makes resolution of the motion a matter of some consequence, Mr. Feldman has failed to show that the Carlson firm acted improperly and, even if it did, this motion must be denied as untimely. Mr. Feldman has not shown that any of the conversations he had with Melissa Dubbs in January 2014 were outside the scope of the authorization that Ms. Dubbs had good cause to believe was given by Mr. Feldman's then-attorney of record Robert Machado and agreed to by Mr. Feldman. By January 2014, although himself not an attorney, Mr. Feldman can fairly be characterized as an experienced litigant who surely knew that anything he said to opposing counsel might be used against him and others with whom he was allied in pending litigation. The motion is untimely. Mr. Feldman knew or could have known all the facts on which he bases this motion and presented them to his new counsel no later than the summer of 2014, yet he waited almost four years to bring this motion and now seeks to undo the work done by the Carlson firm for the last four years and require new counsel to learn all that was done in the last four years, all to the significant prejudice of the Carlson firm's clients. Mr. Feldman has not presented any cogent rationale for his delay in bringing this motion. "Where the party opposing the motion can demonstrate prima facie evidence of unreasonable delay in bringing the motion causing prejudice to the present client, disqualification should not be ordered. The burden then shifts back to the party seeking disqualification to justify the delay." (Western Continental Operating Co. v. Natural Gas Corp.(1989) 212 Cal.App.3d 752, 763.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for Ropers is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CANDLESTICK VIEW HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, VS. J.L. BUILDERS, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant The Siding Specialists Inc. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 21, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANT THE SIDING SPECIALISTS INC'S., MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant The Siding Specialists Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. The undisputed facts show that the claims alleged against Siding Specialists in plaintiff Candlestick View Homeowners Association's second amended complaint are substantially identical to the claims that Candlestick HOA alleged against Lindstrom & Son Builders in the arbitration proceedings. Per claim preclusion principles, the confirmed arbitration award precludes the Candlestick HOA from litigating these claims in this action. (Thibodeau v. Crum (1992) 4 Cal.App.4th 749). Claim preclusion applies regardless of whether Candlestick HOA could have brought any claims against Siding Specialists in arbitration proceedings. Cal Sierra Development, Inc. v. George Reed, Inc. (2017) 14 Cal.App.5th 663 establishes that a party in privity with a party in an arbitration proceeding (such as Siding Specialists who was in privity with Lindstrom) is able to avail itself of claim preclusion against a third party (Candlestick HOA) who arbitrated claims against its privy (Lindstrom). Because Candlestick HOA had a full and fair opportunity to arbitrate all of the claims against Lindstrom that it now seeks to assert against Siding Specialists, there is nothing unfair or inequitable about denying Candlestick HOA a "second bite at the apple." Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Siding Specialists is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: QIAN & NEMECEK LLP VS. YUNJI WILLA QIAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Access To And Copying Of Documents Books And Records Qian And Nemecek Llp And For Monetary Issue Evidence And Claim Cause Of Action Preclusion Terminating Sanctions Against Qian And Nemecek Llp And David Nemecek Jr
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 21, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANT YUNJI QIAN's Motion To Compel Access To And Copying Of Documents Books And Records Qian And Nemecek Llp And For Monetary Issue Evidence And Claim Cause Of Action Preclusion Terminating Sanctions Against Qian And Nemecek Llp And David Nemecek Jr. Pro Tem Judge Stephen Zollman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to Compel Access To And Copying Of Documents Books And Records Qian And Nemecek Llp Compliance is to be completed within ten (10) days of the date of this order. Parties are ordered to check in with the clerk, meet and confer about what discovery is still outstanding and then notify the clerk with they are finished. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to szollman1@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DE XIN LI VS. YU KWAN CONSTRUCTION CO. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendants Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 21, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANT M & D LAU CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant M&D Lau Corporation's motion for summary judgement is granted. M&D satisfied its summary judgment burden of showing that it did not directly hire and was not the employer of either plaintiff De Xin Lin or the person who dropped the metal plate and did not have any actual or constructive knowledge of any dangerous condition regarding the metal plate. Plaintiff did not submit any evidence creating a triable dispute as to who hired or employed plaintiff or the person who dropped the metal plate or M&D's knowledge. The undisputed facts establish that M&D Lau hired Yu Kwan Construction Co., who in turn employed plaintiff and the person who dropped the metal plate and M&D did not retain control over the work performed by plaintiff and the person who dropped the metal plate. Per Privette and its progeny, M&D owed no duty to plaintiff with respect to his working conditions. Plaintiff's request for a continuance of the hearing to take discovery per CCP 437c(h) is denied because plaintiff fails to show that any evidence may exist to allow him to defeat this motion and, in all events, plaintiff has not shown any diligence in seeking to obtain any such evidence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for M&D is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 21, 2018, Line 11, DEFENDANT MACY'S WEST STORES, INC.'S, DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Macy's West Stores, Inc.'s demurrer to both causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Arlene R. Brown is overruled as to both causes of action. Liberally construed, the FAC adequately alleges claims for negligence and premises liability. The issue of whether the non-operating escalator used by Ms. Brown as a staircase was in a dangerous condition requires factual development and is not amenable to resolution on a demurrer.
